era_context: Early Iron Age II. Shalmaneser III's reign continues to dominate Near Eastern politics. The Western Zhou dynasty in China is in its final years, approaching the traditional date of collapse (771 BCE). Greek city-states are consolidating their political institutions and continuing alphabet adoption. Egypt remains fragmented.

events.title: Shalmaneser III's military campaigns in the Levant
events.description: Shalmaneser III continues his military campaigns to expand Assyrian control and extract tribute from Levantine states. These campaigns are documented in royal inscriptions and form part of the broader Neo-Assyrian imperial expansion that characterizes the 9th century BCE.

sources.name: Shalmaneser III's royal inscriptions and palace reliefs

events.certainty_note: Shalmaneser III's reign is well-attested. Specific annual attribution is approximate, based on the known pattern of his campaigns.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are documented for 795 BCE. The entry reflects the continuation of known patterns rather than a documented event.
historiographic_note: 795 BCE is not marked by any specific documented event in surviving sources. The historical record is sparse and relies on inference from the broader pattern of Shalmaneser III's reign. This year, like most ancient years, lacks annual-level specificity in the historical record.

graph_edges.note: Continuation of Shalmaneser III's military program
